name: Sierra de Sant Salvador – circular from Santa Anna Dam
name_original: Sierra de Sant Salvador – circular desde la Presa de Santa Anna
region_intro: Hiking around Castillonroy offers diverse landscapes, from narrow gorges with limestone walls to rolling hills and agricultural areas characteristic of the pre-Pyrenean region. The area features natural attractions such as the Congosto de Baldellou and the Pantano de Santa Ana reservoir. These varied terrains provide opportunities for exploring both natural beauty and historical sites on foot. Best hiking trails around Castillonroy The most popular hiking route is Baldellou Gorge Trail, an easy 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 27 minutes to complete. This route features impressive limestone walls and a winding stream. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sierra de Sant Salvador – circular from Santa Anna Dam, a moderate 6.4 miles (10.3 km) path. This trail offers views of the Santa Ana Reservoir and traverses pre-Pyrenean terrain. Local hikers also love the Ermita de Sant Salvador loop from Montpedró, a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) trail leading through rolling hills and past the historical Ermita de Sant Salvador, often completed in about 2 hours 17 minutes. Hiking around Castillonroy is defined by limestone gorges, rolling hills, and reservoir views. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate treks. The routes in Castillonroy are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Castillonroy's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Castillonroy?
A: Castillonroy offers a wide selection of hiking routes, with over 20 trails to explore. These include 9 easy routes and 15 moderate options, ensuring there's something for various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Castillonroy?
A: Yes, Castillonroy has 9 easy hiking trails. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938179922/baldellou-gorge-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baldellou Gorge Trail&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.1-mile route that takes about 1 hour 27 minutes to complete, featuring impressive limestone walls and a winding stream.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Castillonroy?
A: Hiking in Castillonroy offers diverse landscapes, from the narrow limestone walls of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2450973" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baldellou Gorge&lt;/a&gt; to rolling hills and agricultural areas characteristic of the pre-Pyrenean region. You'll also find natural attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1132485" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Ana Reservoir and Dam&lt;/a&gt;, providing picturesque water views.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Castillonroy?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e947200420/sierra-de-sant-salvador-circular-from-santa-anna-dam" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Sant Salvador – circular from Santa Anna Dam&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 6.4-mile path offering views of the Santa Ana Reservoir and traversing pre-Pyrenean terrain.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I visit while hiking in Castillonroy?
A: You can combine your hikes with visits to several historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3011545" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Salvador Hermitage&lt;/a&gt; is a peaceful spot often incorporated into routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3981615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Sant Salvador loop from Montpedró&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1621342" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Mare de Déu de Cérvoles&lt;/a&gt; and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5061832" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baldellou Village&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region offers several scenic spots. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6313950" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Picons Downhill Trail&lt;/a&gt; provides excellent views of the Santa Ana Reservoir. Additionally, the general pre-Pyrenean terrain and gorges offer numerous panoramic vistas.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Castillonroy?
A: The hiking routes in Castillonroy are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from impressive gorges to rolling hills, and the opportunities to combine nature with historical exploration.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by water features like reservoirs or rivers?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1132485" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Ana Reservoir and Dam&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent feature in the area, with routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e947200420/sierra-de-sant-salvador-circular-from-santa-anna-dam" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Sant Salvador – circular from Santa Anna Dam&lt;/a&gt; offering views of its waters. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2450973" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baldellou Gorge&lt;/a&gt; also features a winding stream.

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty hikes for those looking for more of a challenge?
A: Yes, Castillonroy has 15 moderate hiking trails. For a more challenging experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1397392" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baldellou Gorge – Baldellou Village loop from Baldellou / Valdellou&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 8.8 km and involves more elevation gain.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Castillonroy?
A: While specific seasonal recommendations are not provided, the diverse landscapes of Castillonroy, including gorges and rolling hills, suggest that spring and autumn would be ideal for comfortable temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer might be warm, while winter could offer a different, quieter experience.

Q: Are there any hikes that explore the Baldellou Gorge area in more detail?
A: Yes, the Baldellou Gorge is a key natural feature. Beyond the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938179922/baldellou-gorge-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baldellou Gorge Trail&lt;/a&gt;, you can also explore the area with the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1397395" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baldellou Village – Baldellou Gorge loop from Baldellou / Valdellou&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 6.9 km route that allows for a deeper immersion into the gorge's impressive limestone formations.
tips: Nice half day tour, great all-round view. Also easy to do with children.
View over the Santa Ana reservoir. A good vantage point on one side of the Santa Ana dam and on the other the Alfarrås plain.
